title: Proof by Contradiction
description: A failed debunk tells you what the mix is NOT. Use that, plus the aspect clues, to nail down the answer by elimination.

hints.text: Ingredient 4 is a known alchemical. Ingredient 2 has R−. List out all possible results for mix(ing2, ing4) given these two facts.

Ingredient 4 is alchemical NNN (all Large Negative). Both ingredient 2 and ingredient 4 have R−, so R is a candidate colour in the result. But G could also dominate depending on ingredient 2's G aspect. The failed debunk eliminates one of the two candidates.
With ing2 R− matching ing4 R−, mix(2,4) can only produce R− or G−. The failed debunk proves mix(2,4) ≠ R−. By elimination, the answer must be G−.
